text: The robot is docked on the patient's backside with active arms number one, two, and three, and arm number four canceled.
Like in every robotic thoracic surgery, the instruments for VATS and open thoracic surgery are prepared in the operating room.
A 4 cm intercostal incision is done on the 7th intercostal space between anterior and middle axillary lines.
First, the identification of the two pulmonary veins is performed with the intention of left upper lobectomy.
A unique vein is excluded.

The tumoral invasion of the lung hilum is evaluated and lymph nodes from the hilar, subaortic and subcarinal stations are extracted during the assessment of the tumoral invasion and vascular dissection.
Due to the pulmonary artery invasion from the interior mediastinum to the fissure by the bulk intrapulmonary adenopathy, a left pneumonectomy is imposed.
From the anterior mediastinum, the superior vein is first prepared, encircled with a vessel loop using the tip-up instrument and stapled.
The pulmonary artery is approached with the same steps, dissection around with the tip-up, vessel loop and stapling.
The inferior vein is then dissected and stapled after sectioning the pulmonary ligament.
The last is the left main bronchus, which is dissected and stapled.
After disconnection, the lung is extracted in endobag.
Oncological resection is completed with the mediastinal lymph node dissection in subaortic, paraortic, subcarinal and pulmonary ligament stations.
In new rats pneumonectomy, the TPAP instrument is extremely useful, being long and blunt enough to dissect the hidden part of the vessels and bronchus and pass around as safely as possible.
The TPAP with its robotic wrist successfully and satisfactorily replaces the vats or open
for the main pulmonary artery and bronchus.
Robotic surgeons are able to safely use this instrument after getting used to the lack of haptic feedback.
The most important benefit of the U-rats in pneumonectomy is probably that on the left side it allows the optimal exposure and stapling of the main bronchus as proximal as possible.
The shorter bronchial stump is possible because the angle of stapling is more appropriate when compared to U-vats, the U-rats intercostal incision being placed lower.
So we recommend the 7th intercostal space uniportal axis for pure U-rats pneumonectomy.
The incision should be no less than 4 cm since through it the entire lung will be extracted.

Especially if lymph node dissection is necessary, the deep 3D robotic view and the fine precise and clean dissection with the robotic instruments make the uniportal robotic radical pneumonectomy an excellent option for uniportal surgeons.
